concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: On Page 2, the document includes a textual legend for the 'North Carolina LiDAR Quality Level By Phase' map. This legend contains crucial information defining the five phases and their respective quality levels (e.g., 'Phase 1 (2020) - QL 1', etc.). While the legend is included as an image element (index 19), the text itself has not been extracted into the JSON elements. Given the limited amount of text on Page 2, this omission represents significantly more than 25% of the machine-readable text

In the output (index 26), the first row (row 0) contains only one cell with a colspan of 1, which breaks the alignment with the three columns defined in the subsequent rows. concerns.analysis: There is a significant visual duplication of body content at the end of the HTML document. The entire content block from the third page of the PDF—including the 'Project Timeline' text, the 'Table 5' caption, the data table itself, and the concluding note about quality control—appears twice in immediate succession. Each instance is also preceded by a redundant DPS Emergency Management logo.

On Page 3, the caption for Table 5 (Element index 25) is truncated, ending at 'and', whereas the source contains additional descriptive text ('deliverables should be available by beginning of:').
